summary: EXP-330 closes ISS-1880 with a single-patch deep-attack on data/wins.json ["056"].detail_verdict_text. The generic post-hoc rider is replaced with a WIN-056-specific paragraph that names the actual failure mode: direct parameter import with form-substitution (the dome imports 23.45° as a numerical input at raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25 and adopts the textbook sinusoidal seasonal formula verbatim — Duffie & Beckman 1980 — rather than deriving either from H(r) or any dome parameter). Kernel of truth acknowledged (H(r) construction is sophisticated curve-fitting; deriving observables from internal parameters is the right posture). Verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged. Preserves opening sentence and closing dome-author-definition + Part 3.1b cross-reference verbatim. Patch is paragraph-internal text replacement; old_string verified to appear exactly once in WIN-056.detail_verdict_text; preview apply gives expected output. Tests/run.sh unaffected (no numeric data values touched). NOTE FOR DECIDER: WIN-012, WIN-030, WIN-069 also carry the same generic template phrase — explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this EXP; each requires separate per-WIN inspection (the template may legitimately fit some).
notes: PROP-029 safety net not fired this run (orphan check returned 2 Opus-owned orphans, but Mode 1 entered via tracker pending list — orphans will be picked up subsequently). PROP-034 baby-ownership boundary respected: this is a deep-attack (declared review_class on tracker entry pre-run), Opus-owned correctly.
